query: any where CommandLine like~ ("* -name .htpasswd*", "* -perm -4000 *")

name: SIGMA - Potential Suspicious Change To Sensitive/Critical Files
author: @d4ns4n_ (Wuerth-Phoenix)
description: Detects changes of sensitive and critical files. Monitors files that you don't expect to change without planning on Linux system.
false_positives: Some false positives are to be expected on user or administrator machines. Apply additional filters as needed.
query: any where (((Image like~ ("*/cat", "*/echo", "*/grep", "*/head", "*/more", "*/tail")) and CommandLine:"*>*") or (Image like~ ("*/emacs", "*/nano", "*/sed", "*/vi", "*/vim"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*/bin/login*", "*/bin/passwd*", "*/boot/*", "*/etc/*.conf*", "*/etc/cron.*", "*/etc/crontab*", "*/etc/hosts*", "*/etc/init.d*", "*/etc/sudoers*", "*/opt/bin/*", "*/sbin*", "*/usr/bin/*", "*/usr/local/bin/*"))

name: SIGMA - Shell Execution Of Process Located In Tmp Directory
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects execution of shells from a parent process located in a temporary (/tmp) directory
query: any where ParentImage:"/tmp/*" and (Image like~ ("*/bash", "*/csh", "*/dash", "*/fish", "*/ksh", "*/sh", "*/zsh"))

name: SIGMA - Execution Of Script Located In Potentially Suspicious Directory
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects executions of scripts located in potentially suspicious locations such as "/tmp" via a shell such as "bash", "sh", etc.
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/bash", "*/csh", "*/dash", "*/fish", "*/ksh", "*/sh", "*/zsh")) and CommandLine:"* -c *" and CommandLine:"*/tmp/*"

name: SIGMA - Mask System Power Settings Via Systemctl
author: Milad Cheraghi, Nasreddine Bencherchali
description:
Detects the use of systemctl mask to disable system power management targets such as suspend, hibernate, or hybrid sleep.
Adversaries may mask these targets to prevent a system from entering sleep or shutdown states, ensuring their malicious processes remain active and uninterrupted.
This behavior can be associated with persistence or defense evasion, as it impairs normal system power operations to maintain long-term access or avoid termination of malicious activity.
query: any where (Image:"*/systemctl" and CommandLine:"* mask*") and (CommandLine like~ ("*suspend.target*", "*hibernate.target*", "*hybrid-sleep.target*"))

name: SIGMA - System Information Discovery
author: Ömer Günal, oscd.community
description: Detects system information discovery commands
query: any where Image like~ ("*/uname", "*/hostname", "*/uptime", "*/lspci", "*/dmidecode", "*/lscpu", "*/lsmod")

name: SIGMA - System Network Connections Discovery - Linux
author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
description: Detects usage of system utilities to discover system network connections
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/who", "*/w", "*/last", "*/lsof", "*/netstat")) and (not (ParentCommandLine:"*/usr/bin/landscape-sysinfo*" and Image:"*/who"))

name: SIGMA - System Network Discovery - Linux
author: Ömer Günal and remotephone, oscd.community
description: Detects enumeration of local network configuration
query: any where (Image like~ ("*/firewall-cmd", "*/ufw", "*/iptables", "*/netstat", "*/ss", "*/ip", "*/ifconfig", "*/systemd-resolve", "*/route")) or CommandLine:"*/etc/resolv.conf*"

name: SIGMA - Touch Suspicious Service File
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects usage of the "touch" process in service file.
query: any where Image:"*/touch" and CommandLine:"* -t *" and CommandLine:"*.service"

name: SIGMA - Triple Cross eBPF Rootkit Execve Hijack
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects execution of a the file "execve_hijack" which is used by the Triple Cross rootkit as a way to elevate privileges
query: any where Image:"*/sudo" and CommandLine:"*execve_hijack*"

name: SIGMA - Triple Cross eBPF Rootkit Install Commands
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects default install commands of the Triple Cross eBPF rootkit based on the "deployer.sh" script
query: any where Image:"*/sudo" and (CommandLine:"* tc *" and CommandLine:"* enp0s3 *") and (CommandLine like~ ("* qdisc *", "* filter *"))

name: SIGMA - User Has Been Deleted Via Userdel
author: Tuan Le (NCSGroup)
description: Detects execution of the "userdel" binary. Which is used to delete a user account and related files. This is sometimes abused by threat actors in order to cover their tracks
query: any where Image:"*/userdel"

name: SIGMA - User Added To Root/Sudoers Group Using Usermod
author: TuanLe (GTSC)
description: Detects usage of the "usermod" binary to add users add users to the root or suoders groups
query: any where Image:"*/usermod" and (CommandLine like~ ("*-aG root*", "*-aG sudoers*"))

name: SIGMA - Vim GTFOBin Abuse - Linux
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects the use of "vim" and it's siblings commands to execute a shell or proxy commands.
Such behavior may be associated with privilege escalation, unauthorized command execution, or to break out from restricted environments.
query: any where ((Image like~ ("*/rvim", "*/vim", "*/vimdiff")) and (CommandLine like~ ("* --cmd*", "* -c *"))) and (CommandLine like~ ("*:!/*", "*:lua *", "*:py *", "*/bin/bash*", "*/bin/dash*", "*/bin/fish*", "*/bin/sh*", "*/bin/zsh*"))

name: SIGMA - Linux Webshell Indicators
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious sub processes of web server processes
false_positives: Web applications that invoke Linux command line tools
query: any where ((ParentImage like~ ("*/httpd", "*/lighttpd", "*/nginx", "*/apache2", "*/node", "*/caddy")) or (ParentCommandLine:"*/bin/java*" and ParentCommandLine:"*tomcat*") or (ParentCommandLine:"*/bin/java*" and ParentCommandLine:"*websphere*")) and (Image like~ ("*/whoami", "*/ifconfig", "*/ip", "*/bin/uname", "*/bin/cat", "*/bin/crontab", "*/hostname", "*/iptables", "*/netstat", "*/pwd", "*/route"))

name: SIGMA - Download File To Potentially Suspicious Directory Via Wget
author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
description: Detects the use of wget to download content to a suspicious directory
query: any where Image:"*/wget" and (CommandLine regex~ "\s-O\s" or CommandLine:"*--output-document*") and CommandLine:"*/tmp/*"

name: SIGMA - Potential Xterm Reverse Shell
author: @d4ns4n_
description: Detects usage of "xterm" as a potential reverse shell tunnel
query: any where Image:"*xterm*" and CommandLine:"*-display*" and CommandLine:"*:1"

name: SIGMA - PwnKit Local Privilege Escalation
author: Sreeman
description: Detects potential PwnKit exploitation CVE-2021-4034 in auth logs
query: any where "pkexec" and "The value for environment variable XAUTHORITY contains suspicious content" and "[USER=root] [TTY=/dev/pts/0]"

name: SIGMA - Relevant ClamAV Message
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects relevant ClamAV messages
query: any where "Trojan*FOUND" or "VirTool*FOUND" or "Webshell*FOUND" or "Rootkit*FOUND" or "Htran*FOUND"

name: SIGMA - Modifying Crontab
author: Pawel Mazur
description: Detects suspicious modification of crontab file.
query: any where "REPLACE"

name: SIGMA - Guacamole Two Users Sharing Session Anomaly
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious session with two users present
query: any where "(2 users now present)"

name: SIGMA - SSHD Error Message CVE-2018-15473
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects exploitation attempt using public exploit code for CVE-2018-15473
query: any where "error: buffer_get_ret: trying to get more bytes 1907 than in buffer 308 [preauth]"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious OpenSSH Daemon Error
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious SSH / SSHD error messages that indicate a fatal or suspicious error that could be caused by exploiting attempts
query: any where "unexpected internal error" or "unknown or unsupported key type" or "invalid certificate signing key" or "invalid elliptic curve value" or "incorrect signature" or "error in libcrypto" or "unexpected bytes remain after decoding" or "fatal: buffer_get_string: bad string" or "Local: crc32 compensation attack" or "bad client public DH value" or "Corrupted MAC on input"

name: SIGMA - Sudo Privilege Escalation CVE-2019-14287 - Builtin
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects users trying to exploit sudo vulnerability reported in CVE-2019-14287
query: any where USER like~ ("#-*", "#*4294967295")

name: SIGMA - Disabling Security Tools - Builtin
author: Ömer Günal, Alejandro Ortuno, oscd.community
description: Detects disabling security tools
query: any where "stopping iptables" or "stopping ip6tables" or "stopping firewalld" or "stopping cbdaemon" or "stopping falcon-sensor"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Named Error
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious DNS error messages that indicate a fatal or suspicious error that could be caused by exploiting attempts
query: any where " dropping source port zero packet from " or " denied AXFR from " or " exiting (due to fatal error)"

name: SIGMA - Suspicious VSFTPD Error Messages
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious VSFTPD error messages that indicate a fatal or suspicious error that could be caused by exploiting attempts
query: any where "Connection refused: too many sessions for this address." or "Connection refused: tcp_wrappers denial." or "Bad HTTP verb." or "port and pasv both active" or "pasv and port both active" or "Transfer done (but failed to open directory)." or "Could not set file modification time." or "bug: pid active in ptrace_sandbox_free" or "PTRACE_SETOPTIONS failure" or "weird status:" or "couldn't handle sandbox event" or "syscall * out of bounds" or "syscall not permitted:" or "syscall validate failed:" or "Input line too long." or "poor buffer accounting in str_netfd_alloc" or "vsf_sysutil_read_loop"
